extraction.one_line_summary: HERRADA Jesus won from 26-rider breakaway; EVENEPOEL Remco probed KUSS Sepp with 200m acceleration on steep finish, Kuss matched easily.
tactical_patterns: THOMAS Geraint placed 5th from breakaway despite being best-placed GC rider at 91:4 deficit, suggesting form collapse.
EVENEPOEL Remco tested KUSS Sepp only in final 200m on 14% gradients, revealing tactical passivity despite time trial strength.
CARTHY Hugh exploited Quick-Step's tolerance for lower GC threats escaping, forcing temporary chase response before being reabsorbed.
Quick-Step controlled entire stage with HESSINK Robert, VAN BAARLE Dylan, TRATNIK Jan on flat, then allowed 6-minute breakaway gap.

rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: matched EVENEPOEL's late acceleration effortlessly after time trial, suggesting superior recovery or Remco withholding effort
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: probed only symbolically in final 200m despite stage profile favoring explosive efforts, possibly saving for Tourmalet
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: finished 5th from advantaged breakaway position, confirming abysmal Vuelta form continues
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: neutral: crashed in neutral section requiring medical car visit, but finished on same time as GC group
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: positive: opportunistically attacked when Quick-Step let lower threats escape, forcing tactical response
